message:
feat(model): migrate timestamp fields from Option<String> to Option<DateTime<Utc>>

Replace stringly-typed timestamp fields in Issue, Dependency, and Comment
with proper chrono::DateTime<Utc> values. This eliminates an entire class
of runtime parsing errors by pushing RFC 3339 deserialization to the serde
boundary rather than scattering parse_rfc3339/parse_timestamp calls across
every consumer.

Affected fields:
- Issue: created_at, updated_at, due_date, closed_at
- Dependency: created_at
- Comment: created_at

Also adds two public helpers:
- parse_timestamp(s) -> Option<DateTime<Utc>>: safe RFC 3339 parser for
  production code that needs to convert raw strings.
- ts(s) -> Option<DateTime<Utc>>: panicking convenience for test fixtures,
  so tests can write `ts("2026-01-01T00:00:00Z")` instead of manual parse
  + unwrap chains.

This is a breaking internal API change — all downstream consumers that
previously called `.as_deref()` or `.as_ref()` on these fields to get
`&str` must now work with DateTime<Utc> directly or call `.to_rfc3339()`
when string output is needed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -40,13 +40,13 @@ pub struct Issue {
     #[serde(default)]
     pub estimated_minutes: Option<i32>,
     #[serde(default)]
-    pub created_at: Option<String>,
+    pub created_at: Option<DateTime<Utc>>,
     #[serde(default)]
-    pub updated_at: Option<String>,
+    pub updated_at: Option<DateTime<Utc>>,
     #[serde(default)]
-    pub due_date: Option<String>,
+    pub due_date: Option<DateTime<Utc>>,
     #[serde(default)]
-    pub closed_at: Option<String>,
+    pub closed_at: Option<DateTime<Utc>>,
     #[serde(default)]
     pub labels: Vec<String>,
     #[serde(default)]
@@ -74,7 +74,7 @@ pub struct Dependency {
     #[serde(default)]
     pub created_by: String,
     #[serde(default)]
-    pub created_at: Option<String>,
+    pub created_at: Option<DateTime<Utc>>,
 }
 
 #[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, Default)]
@@ -88,7 +88,7 @@ pub struct Comment {
     #[serde(default)]
     pub text: String,
     #[serde(default)]
-    pub created_at: Option<String>,
+    pub created_at: Option<DateTime<Utc>>,
 }
 
 impl Dependency {
@@ -105,6 +105,25 @@ impl Dependency {
     }
 }
 
+/// Parse an RFC 3339 timestamp string into `DateTime<Utc>`.
+///
+/// Accepts both `"2025-01-10T10:00:00Z"` and `"2025-01-10T10:00:00+00:00"`.
+pub fn parse_timestamp(s: &str) -> Option<DateTime<Utc>> {
+    DateTime::parse_from_rfc3339(s)
+        .ok()
+        .map(|dt| dt.with_timezone(&Utc))
+}
+
+/// Shorthand to create `Some(DateTime<Utc>)` from an RFC 3339 string.
+/// Panics if the string is invalid — intended for test fixtures.
+pub fn ts(s: &str) -> Option<DateTime<Utc>> {
+    Some(
+        DateTime::parse_from_rfc3339(s)
+            .unwrap_or_else(|e| panic!("invalid timestamp {s:?}: {e}"))
+            .with_timezone(&Utc),
+    )
+}
+
 impl Issue {
     #[must_use]
     pub fn normalized_status(&self) -> String {
